app.title: Minecraft Server Manager
server.empty: No servers yet. Click "Add server" to add one.
confirm.delete: Remove "{name}"? (This only removes it from the list; the server's actual data is not deleted.)
add.title: Add server
add.hint: Pick the server's data folder (the one containing server.properties).
add.not_mc: This folder is not a Minecraft server
detect.title: Detection result
detect.rcon_none: RCON port not published yet (needed before phase 4 commands)
log.title: Live log
log.placeholder: Select a server to view its live log.
log.autoscroll_hint: Auto-scroll to the bottom on new log lines; pauses when you scroll up.
control.title: Server control
control.stop_hint: Stop the container (docker stop); online players will be disconnected after the server saves and shuts down.
control.restart_hint: Restart the container (docker restart = stop then start), handy when applying config changes.
confirm.stop: Stop "{name}"? Online players will be disconnected.
confirm.restart: Restart "{name}"? Online players will be disconnected during the restart.
cfg.edit_hint: Edit this server's server.properties (all settings, with explanations).
cfg.title: Edit server.properties
cfg.restart_note: Most changes take effect after a server restart.
cfg.saved_restart: Saved. Most settings need a restart to take effect. Restart the server now?
cfg.empty: This file has no editable settings.
cfg.unsaved: You have unsaved changes that haven't been saved. Leave anyway? Changes will be lost.
list.note: Editable while the server is stopped; changes take effect on next start.
list.locked: The server is running, so these lists are managed by the server and cannot be edited directly. Stop the server first, or use commands in phase 4 (whitelist add / op / ban).
list.locked_note: Server is running; lists are read-only.
list.dup: This entry is already in the list.
list.src.offline: Offline mode: UUID generated by algorithm.
list.field.name: Name
cmd.title: Command console
cmd.placeholder: Type a command, e.g. list, time set day
cmd.hint: Sent via RCON (no leading slash); server must be running with RCON enabled. Use up/down for command history.
